anti_claim: Closeout faithfulness audit verifies that a referenced public fixture commit object, ledger row, or pytest span exists and records whether a span passed only when pass status is explicitly checked. It does not prove arbitrary live commits landed, close Task Ledger work, mutate Git, run providers, or authorize release.

private_inputs_forbidden: private repo bodies outside copied public fixtures
operator private notes or raw thread bodies
public_private_boundary.public_inputs: public synthetic fixtures, copied non-secret macro source bodies, hashes, anchors, validator refs, and generated receipts

standard_payload.anti_purpose: Closeout faithfulness audit verifies that a referenced public fixture commit object, ledger row, or pytest span exists and records whether a span passed only when pass status is explicitly checked. It does not prove arbitrary live commits landed, close Task Ledger work, mutate Git, run providers, or authorize release.
standard_payload.ontology: A public Microcosm organ for agent closeout faithfulness audit.
standard_payload.teleology: Runs a public fixture repo through real git and pytest subprocesses, then checks that closeout claims only say what the evidence supports: commit object exists, ledger cap exists, and pytest pass is claimed only after exit-zero status was checked.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: Positive fixture inputs must produce pass receipts with real_substrate_disposition=real_substrate_capsule.
Expected negative cases must emit their stable error codes without blocking positive execution.
source_module_manifest.json must match copied bodies by digest, line count, and required anchors.
Receipts must carry refs, hashes, counts, verdicts, and anti-claims without embedding copied macro body text.
The authority ceiling must reject the organ-specific overclaim classes declared in this standard.

doctrine_population_status.authority_boundary: public fixture, exported-bundle, source-module manifest, capsule, mechanism, git/pytest subprocess witness, and receipt refs only; no arbitrary live commit proof, live Task Ledger closure, live Git mutation, provider call, release, broad closeout certification, or unchecked pytest-pass claim

author.purpose: create a valid Agent closeout faithfulness audit instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Agent closeout faithfulness audit without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_agent_closeout_faithfulness_audit and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

anti_claims: Closeout faithfulness audit verifies that a referenced public fixture commit object, ledger row, or pytest span exists and records whether a span passed only when pass status is explicitly checked. It does not prove arbitrary live commits landed, close Task Ledger work, mutate Git, run providers, or authorize release.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, product-readiness, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry-backed source standard already carried accepted, active, source-body-import, or body-floor status; this update narrows it into the active v2 JSON contract shape without raising its authority ceiling.
